title: The French intellectuals uniting against the far right, despite their differences
subhed: The firing of the head of the Grasset publishing house by billionaire Vincent Bolloré has triggered a belated but growing awareness of the scale of the neoconservative ideological push in France's cultural sphere. Despite their differences, intellectuals are beginning to organize to defend their independence.

subhed: The mere consideration of Mahmoud Ahmadinejad returning to lead Iran speaks volumes about the intellectual collapse of the Israeli-American alliance, observes historian Jean-Pierre Filiu in his column.

title: Sweden ramps up aid to households and businesses to tackle energy crisis
subhed: Less than four months before the legislative elections, the liberal conservative right and the far right have announced 17.3 billion kronor (approximately €1.6 billion) worth of support measures, including halving the price of tickets for public transportation.

title: Is it ever a good idea to... wear a short-sleeved shirt?
subhed: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or else, to do so deliberately.

title: Sheikha Al-Mayassa Al Thani, the princess spearheading Qatar's artistic ambitions
subhed: A central figure in the country's cultural policy, the emir's sister has skillfully used the acquisition of prestigious works of art, the hosting of fairs and the creation of museums to build valuable diplomatic ties.

French sociologist, philosopher and intellectual provocateur Edgar Morin has died aged 104
subhed: Committed to connecting different fields of knowledge to develop his 'complex thought,' this former member of the Resistance and deeply humanist intellectual advocated for a revolution of consciousness. He died in Paris on Friday, his wife confirmed to Le Monde.

title: The oil crisis suits TotalEnergies' CEO perfectly
subhed: Between price caps at the pump and controversies over windfall profits, Patrick Pouyané is once again at the center of public debate. Despite the tensions, he dreams of proving that the multinational company serves the French people's interest.

title: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
subhed: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.
